task: Redesigned _infer_direction() to use semantic commit-scope analysis instead of root-folder momentum, eliminating false-positive signals that cleared the gate at low confidence
discussion_summary: Identified that Signal 3 (top-level directory frequency) was a design flaw — every commit in any repo touches some top-level folder, making it noise at 0.72 confidence that incorrectly passed the gate. Replaced it with semantic file-change analysis using conventional commit scopes (feat(scope), fix(scope)) parsed from git log. Tested all three signals in isolation: Signal 1 (dirty files) fires at 0.95, Signal 3 (handover next_actions) at 0.85, and the new semantic scope signal correctly identifies lifecycle as the active domain with 0.65 confidence from recent commit history.

accomplishments.what: Rewrote _infer_direction() Signal 3 to parse conventional commit scopes instead of root-folder frequency
Fixed scope regex to correctly extract scope from commit message (removed incorrect ^ anchor)
Validated all three direction signals in isolation with test harness
Committed lifecycle.py changes to main branch

next_actions.action: Run full end-to-end test of _infer_direction() with a fresh session start to verify the three-signal chain produces correct direction inference without HITL gate
next_actions.why: Signals are individually validated but full chain integration needs confirmation before rolling out to production sessions

next_actions.action: Stress-test _infer_direction() against edge cases: empty git log, no conventional commits, all commits in same scope, dirty files in multiple domains
next_actions.why: Robustness required before autonomous sessions rely on this for direction inference

next_actions.action: Update handover prompt in _generate_handover_prompt() to document the three-signal model and confidence thresholds (0.95 for dirty, 0.85 for handover, 0.65 for semantic scope)
next_actions.why: Next session needs to understand how direction was inferred and why confidence levels matter

next_actions.action: Verify HITL gate behavior: confirm that direction_confirm is triggered only when max(signal_confidences) < 0.70, and that it correctly surfaces the three competing signals to the user
next_actions.why: Gate is the safety mechanism preventing low-confidence direction from silently steering the session

decisions.decision: Replaced top-level directory frequency (Signal 3) with semantic commit-scope analysis
decisions.reason: Directory frequency is noise in any repo — every commit touches some top-level folder. Conventional commit scopes (feat(scope), fix(scope)) provide semantic signal about which domain was active in recent work

decisions.decision: Kept Signal 1 (dirty files) and Signal 2 (handover next_actions) unchanged
decisions.reason: Both are high-fidelity: dirty files are immediate context, and handover next_actions are explicit developer intent from the previous session

failed_approaches.approach: Using regex with ^ anchor to match commit scope in git log output
failed_approaches.reason: ^ anchors to start of line, but commit message comes after the hash. Removed anchor to match scope anywhere in the message

relational_files.why: Signal 2 reads next_actions[0] from this file to infer direction from explicit developer intent
_infer_direction() is called by stop.py to populate the direction_confirm gate
